pattern: To shrink the number of distinct values as much as possible, always spend removals on whichever value currently has the fewest copies left.
hints: Only the count of each distinct value matters, not which positions they occupy - start by counting frequencies.
Removing every last copy of the rarest values first is always at least as good as removing copies of a more common value, since it eliminates a whole distinct value for the least cost.

Sort the frequencies ascending and greedily zero out whole groups while k allows it; the moment k can no longer fully clear the next-smallest group, stop - whatever groups remain (fully or partially) is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: several groups of different sizes, removing exactly enough to clear the smallest ones
k is zero, nothing is removed
removing every element leaves zero unique values
a single repeated value with k covering only part of it
k lands exactly between clearing a whole group and only partially clearing the next one
many singleton values compete with one large group for removal priority

stress.description: near-max length with a moderate number of distinct values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 200) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, n)
    return [arr, k]

stress.description: mostly-unique values with a small k relative to length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, size)
    arr = [rng.randint(1, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(0, min(n, 500))
    return [arr, k]
